campbell.title: Campbell City Council — July 7, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a public hearing on July 7, 2026, to consider revoking business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for two smoke shops in the city. The council also discussed adopting a new interim ordinance related to Senate legislation and reviewed consultant service agreements and grant applications.
keyTopics: Revoking business license and tobacco retailer permit for California Cigar
Revoking business license and tobacco retailer permit for Hayes Smoke Shop
Adopting new interim ordinance to supersede prior ordinance
Approving consultant service agreements
Submitting grant application for community project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved preliminary engineering reports and resolutions related to a fiscal year assessment, scheduled an ordinance for adoption on May 20th, and discussed whether the city should explore becoming a charter city. The council also reviewed preliminary estimates for potential tax options and revenue projections.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report preliminary approval for FY
Ordinance adoption scheduled for May 20, 2026
Charter city status exploration and next steps
Preliminary tax and revenue options analysis

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve financial reports, authorize several construction and service contracts, and hear public appeals on unspecified matters following a court order. Key business included reviewing the town's February finances and authorizing the Town Manager to sign agreements with contractors and service providers.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Approved three-year professional services agreement
Heard public appeals following court order (Johnathan Heartnett and others)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met on June 23, 2026, to discuss several items including a proposal to protect a historic building at 647 South Sixth Street, a potential change to how the city fills mayor and council vacancies using ranked choice voting, and decisions about airport advertising and the city's electricity supplier.
keyTopics: Historic building protection at 647 South Sixth Street with landmark designation and tax benefits
Ranked choice voting option for filling mayor and councilmember vacancies
Large format advertising sign contract at San José airport
Long-term electricity supply agreement with Shell Energy North America

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a closed-door session on June 23, 2026 to discuss hiring a new City Attorney and to meet with legal counsel about an ongoing lawsuit involving water quality issues with San Francisco Baykeeper. The council also reviewed a residential development project proposed for 333 Franklin Street.
keyTopics: New City Attorney position being filled
San Francisco Baykeeper lawsuit against Mountain View and Sunnyvale
Residential development planned at 333 Franklin Street
Legal strategy discussions held in closed session

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular public meeting at 7 P.M. The specific topics discussed in the closed session were not disclosed to the public, as is standard practice for confidential council matters.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.
Flag salute and public meeting protocols

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a study session to review and update the Health and Safety section of the city's long-term General Plan. The council also recognized the Parks and Recreation Department for their work across all divisions and celebrated Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad team achievements.
keyTopics: Updating Health and Safety Element of General Plan (covering 2015-2040 vision)
Parks and Recreation Department recognition during Parks and Recreation Month
Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team achievement recognition

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — July 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on July 14, 2026, using both in-person and remote participation options. Residents could attend in person or join via Zoom, and submit written comments before the meeting through the city's online system.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation via Zoom at meeting ID 997-0675-9306
Written public comments accepted through eComment system on City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to update the city's five-year federal housing and community development plan and discuss internal procedural changes. Key items included reviewing budget plans, council member requests for new workplan items, and a proposed change to how council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Update to 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan for federal housing and development funding
Change to Council Vacancy Ordinance
New workplan item requests from individual councilmembers
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council honored Bill Gutoff's retirement and held public hearings on utility rate changes for water, wastewater, and trash collection for the next fiscal year. The Finance Committee recommended approval of seven resolutions adjusting how these essential services are funded.
keyTopics: Water rate schedule changes for fiscal year 2027
Wastewater collection utility rate adjustments
Refuse/trash utility rate modifications
Reserve management practices for utilities
Retirement recognition for Bill Gutoff
